nodes.label: IRIMSV Region V -> LRMIS staging delivery worker.
nodes.norm_label: irimsv region v -> lrmis staging delivery worker.

nodes.label: Graphify Knowledge Graph
nodes.rationale: Scoped subgraph queries (graphify query/path/explain) return much smaller context than GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep; the graph must be refreshed after code changes via `graphify update .` which is AST-only and has no API cost.

nodes.label: Local Development Stack
nodes.rationale: The local stack deliberately mirrors the integration boundary: PostgreSQL (localhost:5433) holds authoritative irimsv data plus integration state, while MySQL (localhost:3307) holds only the LRMIS-owned staging contract. The automation never applies or alters the LRMIS production schema.

nodes.label: Customer Pilot Bootstrap Flow
nodes.rationale: The pilot is explicitly reviewed: initial schema approval (--approve-initial), pilot mapping creation, and mapping approval are separate manual steps, each attributed to a human via --by, so no mapping activates without a person on record.

nodes.label: IRIMSV (Region V Data-Entry System)
nodes.rationale: IRIMSV is the sole Region V data-entry system; its PostgreSQL schema is kept authoritative so the integration only projects outward and never becomes a second source of truth.

nodes.label: Integration Safety Model
nodes.rationale: The service never writes to LRMIS production. The integration schema keeps mapping/schema versions immutable, stores reproducible outbound projections, and deletes publish operation=deactivate with active=false instead of destroying target rows, so every outbound effect is auditable and reversible.

nodes.label: Outbox Events
nodes.rationale: Outbox events live in the integration schema alongside projections, crosswalks, quarantine, retries, drift reports, kill switches, and audit records so publication state is transactional with the source data.

nodes.label: External Reference UUID
nodes.rationale: Every synchronized record gets an immutable external_reference UUID; MySQL delivery uses it as a unique upsert key, which is what makes retries safe.

nodes.label: Idempotent Delivery
nodes.rationale: Idempotent publication tolerates outages, duplicate and out-of-order delivery, and retries without corrupting the LRMIS staging target.

nodes.label: Schema Drift Policy
nodes.rationale: The MySQL contract is normalized and fingerprinted. Added nullable fields are non-breaking; removed tables/columns, datatype or key changes, and newly required fields are breaking. Breaking drift stores a report, disables affected entity controls, and pauses their approved mappings while unaffected entities continue — blast radius is limited to mappings that write to affected LRMIS tables. Later compatible contracts require an explicit approve-schema of the exact fingerprint, and mappings are bound to that fingerprint.

nodes.label: Entity Kill Switch
nodes.rationale: Disabling one entity (e.g. customer) stops only that entity's flow; other entities keep running, so an incident in one contract does not halt the whole integration.

nodes.label: Event Replay
nodes.rationale: A quarantined, dead-letter, or previously delivered event can be reprocessed by UUID; idempotent delivery makes replay of already-delivered events safe.

nodes.label: Quarantine and Dead Letter Queues
nodes.rationale: Non-empty dead_letter/quarantined queues are explicit monitoring signals, alongside disabled entities, oldest pending event age, failed schema checks, and reconciliation differences.

nodes.label: AI Draft Mapping Proposals
nodes.rationale: AI output may create draft mapping proposals only; an administrator must approve them. No process automatically alters LRMIS staging or activates an AI-proposed mapping — human approval is the hard gate on AI-generated schema mappings.

nodes.label: LRMIS Staging Contract
nodes.rationale: Delivery targets an LRMIS-owned MySQL staging database, never LRMIS production. Production requires TLS, private networking or IP allowlisting, a MySQL account limited to SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE on approved staging tables, and a signed contract covering tables, required fields, enums, stable external references, acknowledgement semantics, retention, and privacy.

nodes.label: Customer Pilot Entity
nodes.rationale: The customer entity is only a pilot; the real duplicated teacher workflow should be inventoried and entities added one at a time, reviewed, in dependency order.

nodes.label: Reconciliation
nodes.rationale: Field-level reconciliation between source and target is a required acceptance and monitoring signal, catching divergence that delivery-time checks miss.

nodes.label: scripts/backfill_customers.py
nodes.rationale: Backfill is a repeat-safe initial load routed through the same validation and delivery path as normal events, so bulk loads cannot bypass the safety model.

nodes.label: real_target_db (MySQL 8.0.40 Real-Test Service)
nodes.rationale: Pinned to mysql:8.0.40 to match the server that produced sql/lrmis.sql; MySQL 8.4 rejects one legacy foreign key that the source server accepted.
